Yesterday (Tuesday) - Markets continued to demonstrate resilience as investors looked beyond near-term uncertainty and focused on the broader economic outlook. While trading remained mixed throughout the session, the major indexes held their constructive posture and TurboRegime's RiskOn signal remained firmly intact through the closing bell. The current market environment continues to favor measured participation while avoiding unnecessary reactions to normal day-to-day volatility.

Today (Wednesday) - Pre-market futures are indicating a relatively quiet opening as investors await additional economic data and the latest round of corporate earnings. Overnight trading suggests little change in overall market sentiment, but TurboRegime will continue to rely exclusively on confirmed end-of-day signals before considering any portfolio adjustments. As always, pre-market activity provides useful context but does not influence the model's allocation decisions.

Current Risk Regime - RiskOn remains in effect, active since July 1. Following yesterday's close, the current trade stands at a gain of 0.9%, while the maximum drawdown since entering the position remains just -0.2%. Now entering its tenth trading day, the current regime has exhibited remarkably stable behavior, reinforcing the importance of allowing established market trends to develop rather than reacting to short-term fluctuations. The model continues to monitor market strength and participation for evidence that conditions are either improving toward a potential RiskOn++ opportunity or deteriorating enough to warrant a defensive transition.

Hold or Trade - HOLD. No portfolio changes are indicated following yesterday's close. Current allocation remains 85% SPY, 15% SHV, and 0% SPXL. The existing allocation continues to provide broad market participation while maintaining a modest defensive position. Unless a confirmed regime change occurs at today's close, no action is required.

Live Performance - Live tracking has now reached 106 days since beginning on April 1, 2026. Through yesterday's close, TurboRegime has executed just six live trades while producing a cumulative return of 33.3%, compared with 15.4% for a buy-and-hold investment in SPY over the same period. The strategy continues to outperform SPY by 17.9 percentage points while navigating multiple market environments with only a handful of disciplined allocation changes.

Perhaps equally encouraging, live execution continues to closely mirror historical expectations. Since April 1, the live portfolio has returned 31.8%, compared with 32.4% for the historical backtest over the same period—a variance of just 0.6 percentage points. While small differences between live trading and backtesting are expected because of execution timing and market conditions, the close alignment observed to date provides continued confidence that the strategy is performing as designed in real-world market conditions.

General Commentary - One of the most common investing mistakes is confusing activity with progress. Successful long-term investing does not require frequent trading—it requires making the right decisions when market conditions meaningfully change. TurboRegime is designed to remain patient during periods of market noise while responding decisively when objective evidence supports a regime change. The current RiskOn trade illustrates that philosophy well, remaining invested through normal day-to-day fluctuations while allowing the broader market trend the opportunity to develop.
